## Chronology Spine

## Core Truth Set
- (4/5) Comanche are framed as a disruptive force whose full-plains mobility can rewrite existing system assumptions. | Quote: "Comanche ... disruptive force that rewrites the system ... full plains mobility."
- (4/5) Osage are represented as dominant range holders using trade and conflict assertively to reinforce territorial control. | Quote: "Osage ... dominant range holders ... trade used to assert control and reinforce dominance."
- (4/5) Narrative phase shifts from building influence to sustaining authority under escalating external stress. | Quote: "Story shifts from building power to holding power under stress."
- (4/5) Wichita, Osage, and Comanche are framed as pressure systems that test existing network stability rather than ordinary participants. | Quote: "Wichita -> Osage -> Comanche ... They are pressure systems."
- (4/5) Wichita are described as plains-river hybrids that expand system reach westward through mobile connector trade logic. | Quote: "Wichita ... Plains-River Hybrid ... They expand the system westward."

## Setting Anchors
- Plains-Caddoan Pressure Interface [REGION]: Strategic interface where mobile plains powers and established Caddoan-network actors generate sustained pressure on trust and control systems.

## Continuity Constraints
- Engaging Self-Sufficient Power: Osage-facing power asymmetry constraint.
- Disruption Adaptation Cycle: Comanche-facing adaptation constraint.

## Open Research Gaps
- At what thresholds did Comanche mobility patterns force structural changes in existing alliance and trade systems? (priority 2): Needed to mark the transition from stress to systemic rewrite in chapter arcs.
- What specific mechanisms did Osage use to enforce dominance across wide territorial ranges? (priority 2): Needed for realistic conflict and negotiation stakes under asymmetric power.
- Which route architectures made Wichita connector power structurally distinct from river-centric Caddoan nodes? (priority 2): Needed to model westward expansion with concrete movement/trade mechanics.

## Next Chapter Assembly Rule
- Do not promote a chapter beat to the core story unless it references at least one core claim, one chronology anchor (or explicit open gap), and one continuity constraint.
